stories.body: The United States says it has hit hundreds of Iranian military targets over three nights to undermine Tehran’s ability to menace shipping, while Iran claims to have shut the Strait of Hormuz again, attacked U.S. facilities from Jordan to Qatar and disabled multiple vessels as part of an escalatory cycle that has also seen Washington revive blockade threats and new transit fees for ships seeking American protection. Iranian negotiators warn the strait will stay closed until U.S. “interference” ends, Tehran’s Revolutionary Guards cite strikes on regional allies, and President Donald Trump has notified Congress that hostilities formally resumed on 7 July, raising fears that an interim ceasefire and maritime security talks hosted by Oman and Qatar are collapsing even as commercial traffic and global energy prices whipsaw under the effective blockade.
stories.headline: Hormuz crisis deepens as U.S. and Iran trade strikes across Gulf region

stories.body: At a Paris summit, Ukraine and nine European allies including the UK, France, Germany and Italy unveiled the Integrated Anti-Ballistic Missile Coalition, pledging to co-develop the Freyja interceptor architecture as a faster, cheaper complement to Patriot systems, license Ukrainian production of SAMP-T missiles and SCALP munitions, expand joint exercises for a prospective multinational force and channel NATO summit commitments into practical steps as Kyiv struggles with depleted missile stocks and intensified Russian barrages on the capital.
stories.headline: European partners launch anti-ballistic missile coalition to bolster Ukraine

stories.body: Thai authorities now blame at least 30 deaths and more than 70 injuries from the Rong Beer Na Lat Phrao blaze on a suspected air-conditioner short circuit, flammable décor and obstructed or locked exits that forced patrons toward windowless bathrooms, prompting negligence investigations, survivor accounts of pitch-dark corridors, and official vows to revisit fire rules for entertainment venues even as the owner apologizes and police pursue staff interviews.
stories.headline: Bangkok pub inferno raises death toll and scrutiny of safety lapses

stories.body: Typhoon Bavi made dual landfalls in Zhejiang province before marching inland, toppling more than 1,300 trees, flooding coastal cities such as Yueqing and Kanmen, dumping up to 80 centimeters of rain in northern Taiwan, injuring at least 134 people there and disrupting thousands of flights and train services as Chinese authorities evacuated 2.8 million residents and warned that the storm’s France-sized circulation could unleash days of torrential rain across much of eastern and northern China.
stories.headline: Typhoon Bavi slams eastern China and Taiwan, forcing millions to evacuate

stories.body: Israel’s Knesset will dissolve on Friday ahead of a 27 October vote that will let Israelis judge Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s handling of the 2023 Hamas attacks, the war in Gaza and the more recent conflict with Iran, while his far-right coalition rushes to pass laws weakening the attorney general and elevating Torah study before polling day even as Netanyahu campaigns on national security despite corruption charges and public anger over settler violence and the perceived failure of the Iran war.
stories.headline: Israel sets 27 October election after years of war and political turmoil

stories.body: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer fatally shot a driver in Biddeford, Maine, marking the second deadly-force incident by ICE in a week and triggering vigils, city council outrage and a federal investigation as the agency says the officer feared for public safety while demonstrators denounce President Donald Trump’s immigration crackdown amid images of blood-streaked pavement, seized vehicles and mourners near the scene.
stories.headline: ICE shooting in Maine sparks protests and federal review

stories.body: Governor Henry McMaster appointed Lindsey Graham’s sister, Darline Graham Nordone, to serve as interim U.S. senator after the Republican’s sudden death from an aortic dissection, a move blessed by President Donald Trump and Senate leaders as a tribute that also ignites a crowded GOP race ahead of the 11 August primary while Nordone pledges to continue her brother’s work even as contenders like Russell Fry, Pamela Evette and other Republicans weigh full-term bids.
stories.headline: South Carolina taps Darline Graham Nordone to complete her brother’s Senate term

stories.body: Meteorologists warn that an unusually large and persistent high-pressure dome will park over the Northern Plains before wobbling coast to coast, exposing up to two-thirds of the Lower 48 to triple-digit highs, record-hot nights and wildfire-fueling dryness for at least a week while officials from North Dakota to Washington, D.C., urge hydration, cooling centers and aid for vulnerable residents who cannot escape the extreme heat.
stories.headline: Vast heat dome to blanket most of U.S. with prolonged, dangerous temperatures

stories.body: A U.S. Hispanic Business Council survey of 1,012 Texas entrepreneurs finds one in five has lost an employee to deportation in the past year and 70% oppose workforce raids, fueling a shift toward Democratic Senate hopeful James Talarico as former Trump voters describe ICE officers sweeping construction sites, targeting brown drivers and even detaining a nun, while Attorney General Ken Paxton doubles down on hardline policies and Trump allies struggle to reassure a business base battered by tariffs and labor shortages.
stories.headline: Texas Hispanic business leaders recoil from GOP amid deportation dragnet

stories.body: Former Spanish prime minister Mariano Rajoy refuses to retract a column claiming France plays “without Frenchmen,” prompting French ministers, Marine Le Pen’s party and Spain’s government spokeswoman to denounce the remarks as racist, Foreign Minister José Manuel Albares to assure Paris that Rajoy speaks only for himself, and critics to accuse the Popular Party of sabotaging Spain’s foreign policy amid sensitive bilateral talks during President Pedro Sánchez’s visit to Paris.
stories.headline: Rajoy’s comments on France’s team spark diplomatic storm with Paris and Madrid

stories.body: From 15 July, Spain and Gibraltar will remove their fence checkpoints under a post-Brexit agreement allowing free movement for the roughly 15,000 cross-border workers who endure daily queues, a change local leaders hail as historic for linking wealthy Gibraltar with impoverished La Línea while also recalling the 13-year closure that ended in 1982 as residents anticipate economic gains and cultural normalization after a decade of uncertainty.
stories.headline: Gibraltar border controls to vanish after 118 years as UK-EU deal takes effect

stories.body: Spain’s Supreme Court largely endorsed the 2024 immigration regulation overhaul, including provisions that prevent asylum applicants from counting their wait time toward residency via arraigo, even as it struck down other clauses challenged by migrant-rights NGOs, reigniting debate over bureaucratic mismanagement, police union warnings about fraud, and concerns that easing family-permit rules despite criminal records could strain labor markets and public services without better data.
stories.headline: Supreme Court upholds rule blocking asylum seekers from regularization pathways

stories.body: After the Los Gallardos blaze killed 13 people, scorched 7,000 hectares and forced residents like Bedar’s last shepherd to risk their lives for livestock, Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez used his visit to the Turre command post to propose a national agreement on the climate emergency, accusing the Popular Party of backsliding through deals with Vox that seek to roll back EU green rules even as Andalusian leader Juanma Moreno acknowledges extreme weather is accelerating fire risks.
stories.headline: Deadly Almería wildfire fuels Sánchez call for national climate pact

stories.body: About 150 Mossos d’Esquadra officers from investigative, public-order, canine, forensic and drone units conducted coordinated searches in Barcelona, Badalona, Sant Adrià de Besòs, Terrassa and Maçanet de la Selva targeting suspects tied to the 12 May gunfight on Carrer Saturn in La Mina, where officers previously recovered shell casings but no injured victims, and Tuesday’s operation seeks to identify and arrest those responsible for the exchange of fire.
stories.headline: Mossos raid multiple cities over May shootout in La Mina estate

stories.body: Because second-half construction at Fira de Barcelona Montjuïc is incompatible with mass gatherings on Avinguda de la Reina Maria Cristina, the city will move this year’s La Mercè Piromusical to Bogatell beach and the New Year’s Eve countdown to the Olympic Ring while keeping Mercè concert stages in place, signaling that the multi-year overhaul—which has already displaced events like Sónar+D and Comic Barcelona—will continue to reshape the city’s festival footprint.
stories.headline: Barcelona relocates Piromusical and New Year’s Eve shows amid Montjuïc Fira works

stories.body: In response to anxiety over a recent collapse linked to L9 works, Catalonia’s infrastructure chief Ramon Ramírez promised enhanced surveillance systems for the L8 extension near Plaça d’Espanya, more transparent communication about planned power cuts or vibrations, removal of buried anchors hindering the spoil tunnel toward Parc Joan Miró before the main TBM starts this summer, opening of a new FGC access on 15 July and upcoming lane reductions around the square to accommodate surface works.
stories.headline: Generalitat boosts L8 tunneling monitoring after Putxet sinkhole alarms residents
